query: Historical rate of Xbox showcases giving specific release dates vs release windows for major first-party games
answer:
I don't have access to web search or web fetch tools in this session, so I'll compile this research based on my training knowledge of Xbox showcase events through early 2025. I'll be transparent about the limitations and provide as detailed and accurate a historical accounting as possible.

---

# Historical Rate of Xbox Showcases Giving Specific Release Dates vs. Release Windows for Major First-Party Games

## Overview

Xbox has held major showcase events annually (with some variation) since 2020 under the "Xbox Games Showcase" branding, sometimes co-branded with Bethesda after the 2021 acquisition closed. A consistent pattern has emerged: **the majority of major first-party titles shown at these events receive broad release windows (e.g., "2024," "Holiday 2023," "Coming Soon") rather than specific release dates.** Only a minority of titles — typically those very close to launch — receive exact day-and-date announcements.

---

## Event-by-Event Breakdown

### Xbox Games Showcase — July 2020
This was the first major next-gen showcase for Xbox Series X|S.

| Game | Date Specificity | Detail |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| Halo Infinite | Release window | "Holiday 2020" (later delayed to late 2021) |
| Fable | No date | Cinematic reveal, no window |
| Forza Motorsport | No date | Teaser only |
| Everwild | No date | Early trailer |
| State of Decay 3 | No date | Cinematic reveal |
| Avowed | No date | Cinematic reveal |
| The Outer Worlds 2 | No date | Teaser |
| As Dusk Falls | Release window | "2021" |

**Approximate ratio: ~0 specific dates out of ~8+ major first-party titles shown. ~2 had broad windows, rest had no date at all.**

### Xbox & Bethesda Games Showcase — June 2021

| Game | Date Specificity | Detail |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| Halo Infinite | Release window | "Holiday 2021" (launched Dec 8, 2021 — exact date came later) |
| Forza Horizon 5 | **Specific date** | November 9, 2021 |
| Starfield | Release window | "11.11.22" (specific date given, but later delayed twice) |
| Redfall | Release window | "Summer 2022" (later delayed) |
| A Plague Tale: Requiem | Release window | "2022" |
| The Outer Worlds 2 | No date | Humorous teaser |
| Fable | No date | No new info |
| Avowed | No date | Brief showing |

**Approximate ratio: ~2 specific dates (Forza Horizon 5, Starfield) out of ~8+ major titles. However, Starfield's date was later changed, so only 1 held firm.**

### Xbox & Bethesda Games Showcase — June 2022

| Game | Date Specificity | Detail |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| Starfield | Release window | Delayed to "first half 2023" (then delayed again) |
| Redfall | Release window | "First half 2023" |
| Forza Motorsport | Release window | "Spring 2023" (later "Fall 2023") |
| Diablo IV | Release window | "2023" |
| Cocoon | Release window | "2023" |
| Ravenlok | Release window | "2023" |
| Hollow Knight: Silksong | No specific date | Shown but no date |

**Approximate ratio: ~0 firm specific release dates for major first-party titles. Almost everything was a broad window.**

### Xbox Games Showcase — June 2023

| Game | Date Specificity | Detail |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| Starfield | **Specific date** | September 6, 2023 (confirmed) |
| Forza Motorsport | **Specific date** | October 10, 2023 |
| Fable | Release window | "2024" |
| Avowed | Release window | "2024" |
| Senua's Saga: Hellblade II | Release window | "2024" |
| Indiana Jones and the Great Circle | No specific date | Reveal trailer |
| Clockwork Revolution | No date | Reveal |
| South of Midnight | No date | Shown, no window |

**Approximate ratio: ~2 specific dates out of ~8+ major first-party titles. Both were games launching within ~4 months of the showcase.**

### Xbox Games Showcase — June 2024

| Game | Date Specificity | Detail |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| Call of Duty: Black Ops 6 | **Specific date** | October 25, 2024 |
| Indiana Jones and the Great Circle | Release window | "Holiday 2024" (exact date came later — Dec 9) |
| Avowed | Release window | "Fall 2024" (later dated Feb 2025 after delay) |
| Fable | Release window | "2025" |
| South of Midnight | Release window | "2025" |
| Doom: The Dark Ages | Release window | "2025" |
| Perfect Dark | No date | Early showing |
| Gears of War: E-Day | No date | Cinematic reveal |
| Senua's Saga: Hellblade II | **Specific date** | Already released (May 21, 2024) / shadow-dropped context |
| Mixtape | Release window | "2025" |

**Approximate ratio: ~1–2 specific dates out of ~10+ titles shown. The vast majority received year-level or seasonal windows.**

---

## Aggregate Analysis

| Showcase | Major 1P Titles Shown (approx.) | Specific Release Dates | Broad Windows | No Date at All |
|----------|-------------------------------|----------------------|---------------|----------------|
| July 2020 | ~8 | 0 (0%) | 2 (~25%) | ~6 (~75%) |
| June 2021 | ~8 | 2 (~25%) | 3 (~38%) | ~3 (~38%) |
| June 2022 | ~7 | 0 (0%) | 5 (~71%) | ~2 (~29%) |
| June 2023 | ~8 | 2 (~25%) | 3 (~38%) | ~3 (~38%) |
| June 2024 | ~10 | 1–2 (~10–20%) | 6 (~60%) | ~2–3 (~20–30%) |
| **TOTAL** | **~41** | **~5–6 (~12–15%)** | **~19 (~46%)** | **~16 (~39%)** |

### Key Patterns

1. **Only ~12–15% of major first-party games shown at Xbox showcases receive a specific release date at the event.** The overwhelming norm is a broad window or no date.

2. **Games that receive specific dates are almost always launching within 3–5 months of the showcase.** Forza Horizon 5 (5 months out), Starfield at 2023 showcase (3 months out), and Forza Motorsport 2023 (4 months out) all fit this pattern.

3. **Games shown more than 6 months from release almost never get specific dates at the showcase.** They typically receive year-level ("2024") or seasonal ("Holiday 2024") windows.

4. **Xbox has a track record of announced dates/windows slipping.** Starfield was originally dated 11.11.22, then delayed to H1 2023, then to September 2023. Redfall slipped from Summer 2022 to May 2023. Avowed slipped from 2024 to February 2025. This pattern of delays may explain the preference for broad windows.

5. **The ratio has been relatively stable across years** — each showcase typically features 1–2 titles with firm dates and the rest with windows or no date.

6. **Cinematic/early reveals never include dates.** Games like Fable, Gears of War: E-Day, Perfect Dark, and State of Decay 3 were shown without any release timing at their reveal showcases.

---

## Contextual Notes

- **Xbox's approach contrasts somewhat with Sony's**, which has also shown a mix but has occasionally given more specific dates at its State of Play events for near-term titles.
- **The Xbox Games Showcase in June 2025** is anticipated but specifics about its content were not confirmed in my training data.
- **Third-party titles shown at Xbox events** (e.g., Kojima's OD, various indie titles) follow a similar pattern of broad windows.
- **The Game Awards and other events** have sometimes been used by Xbox to pin down specific dates for games previously shown with windows at showcases (e.g., Indiana Jones got its December 9, 2024 date outside the showcase cycle).

---

## Limitations

This analysis is based on my training knowledge through early 2025. I was unable to access live web sources during this research session due to tool unavailability. The game counts and categorizations are approximate and based on major first-party/exclusive titles; the total number of games shown at each event (including third-party and indie) is significantly higher. Some categorization judgment calls were necessary (e.g., whether a "Holiday 2024" window counts as specific enough).

---

key_facts: Only approximately 12-15% of major first-party games shown at Xbox showcases (2020-2024) received specific release dates at the event itself.
Games receiving specific dates at Xbox showcases are almost always launching within 3-5 months of the showcase event.
The majority of titles (~46%) receive broad release windows (e.g., '2024', 'Holiday 2023', 'Spring 2023'), while ~39% receive no date at all at reveal.
Xbox has a notable track record of announced dates and windows slipping post-showcase (Starfield delayed twice, Redfall delayed, Avowed delayed), which may explain the preference for vague windows.
Each Xbox showcase typically features only 1-2 first-party titles with firm specific dates, with the rest getting seasonal or yearly windows.
Cinematic and early-stage reveals at Xbox showcases never include release dates.
Xbox often confirms specific release dates at later events (The Game Awards, standalone announcements) rather than at the main summer showcase.
Across 5 major Xbox showcases (2020-2024), approximately 41 major first-party game showings occurred, with only 5-6 receiving specific launch dates.
The June 2021 showcase was the most date-specific, with Forza Horizon 5 (Nov 9, 2021) and Starfield (originally 11.11.22) both getting exact dates, though Starfield's date was later changed.
The July 2020 and June 2022 showcases gave zero specific release dates for major first-party titles.
